name: UI Bridge State Machine
description: UI Bridge State Machine page (/automation-builder/ui-bridge-states). Smoke-parity for ui-bridge-graph-editor.spec.ts:47/61 (page loads without a 500; content renders). The page's chrome is project-independent: the 'State Machine' header, the configuration selector, and the six panel-switch buttons (Discovery / Graph Editor / State View / Transitions / Pathfinding / Export) are ALWAYS rendered regardless of project selection (UIBridgeStateMachinePage.tsx:170-236) — the project gate only swaps each panel's BODY between a 'Select a project...' prompt and the live panel. We anchor on that always-present chrome (no ?project= needed), which is the most structurally stable rendering ci-bot produces. Read-only; structural only (no data-driven assertions). Two coherent states: the header anchor and the panel-tab strip.

states.name: Page header
states.description: Top-of-page header anchoring the State Machine page, with the always-present configuration selector. Rendered independent of project selection.

assertions.description: h1 'State Machine' heading anchors the page.
Configuration selector combobox is present in the header (always rendered).

states.name: Panel switch buttons
states.description: The six panel-switch buttons (Discovery, Graph Editor, State View, Transitions, Pathfinding, Export) rendered as a button strip below the header. These are plain <button> elements (not Radix tabs) with stable auto-assigned ids, always present regardless of project selection. Asserting three of the six (the first, a middle, and the last) is sufficient to anchor the strip structurally.

assertions.description: Discovery panel-switch button (first in the strip).
Export panel-switch button (last in the strip).

apiAssertions.description: Pins the canonical UNKNOWN_ROUTE 404 envelope contract for the ui-bridge proxy fallthrough (manual-test-loop iter 1): ANY route not in UI_BRIDGE_ROUTES nor the relay-transport set is short-circuited to {success:false, code:"UNKNOWN_ROUTE", message:"<path> is not a UI Bridge route (no entry in the SDK's UI_BRIDGE_ROUTES manifest or the relay transport set)"} 404 (_browser-required.ts isKnownRoute=false -> unknownRouteResponse), regardless of browser connection. HISTORY: this assertion previously pinned the OLD fallthrough behavior, where unknown routes shared the NO_BROWSER_CONNECTED 503 envelope with browser-required routes — that conflation sent manual-test agents down a no-browser dead-end on typo'd/nonexistent routes, so iter 1 split the two conditions into distinct envelopes (unknown -> 404 UNKNOWN_ROUTE; browser-required without relay client -> 503 NO_BROWSER_CONNECTED, now unit-pinned deterministically in _browser-required.test.ts since spec CI's auto-connected tab makes the 503 path unreachable here). We hit a sentinel unknown path under /control so the assertion is stable on every run. Issued through the same-origin Next proxy (the page's auth path). NOTE: the harness json_path evaluator (api-contract.ts resolveDotPath) uses bare dot-paths WITHOUT the JSONPath `$.` root token, so jsonPath values are `success`/`code`/`message`, not `$.success`. SCHEMA-FIRST: the proxy 404 body conforms_to the canonical UiBridgeHttpErrorEnvelope (qontinui-schemas app_events.rs → corpus schemas.json), which models {success:bool, code:string, message:string}. This is the generic proxy error envelope, distinct from the runner WS/relay UiBridgeResponseEnvelope (that requires requestId/type/timestamp — all absent here, so conforms_to:"UiBridgeResponseEnvelope" correctly reds it). The value-level status/success/code/message asserts are kept as defense-in-depth alongside the whole-body schema check. Provable-red: a regression that stopped translating unknown routes to the structured 404 (bare SDK 404, regression to the shared 503, or dropped `code`/`success`) reds status_code / the json_path / conforms_to assertions; renaming a required field (e.g. code→errorCode) or wrong type reds conforms_to even if the value-level asserts were loosened.

assertions.expected: is not a UI Bridge route

apiAssertions.description: Schema-first C1 contract for the UI-Bridge SDK HTTP health envelope (GET /api/ui-bridge/health). This route is in the SDK's relay-transport set (route.ts RELAY_TRANSPORT_ROUTES /health → isKnownRoute=true), so the proxy forwards it to the SDK's handleRelayRoute handler (ui-bridge nextjs.ts), which returns {success:true, data:{responsive, lastHeartbeat, ...transportDiagnostics}, timestamp, uiBridge:{appId,...,capabilities}} — relay-INDEPENDENT (always 200 regardless of browser connection; the data just reflects current transport state), so the assertion is deterministic on every CI run. Issued through the same-origin Next proxy (the page's auth path). The body conforms_to the canonical UiBridgeHttpHealthEnvelope (qontinui-schemas app_events.rs → corpus schemas.json): success:bool + data:UiBridgeHttpHealthData{responsive:bool, lastHeartbeat:int} + timestamp:int + optional uiBridge:UiBridgeHttpHealthAppInfo. The transport-diagnostics spread (pendingCommandCount, connectedTabs, buildId, …) is allowed because the data schema is open (schemars omits additionalProperties:false). DISTINCT from the runner WS/relay UiBridgeResponseEnvelope (requires requestId/type — absent on the HTTP health envelope, so conforms_to that type would correctly red). Value-level success/responsive-presence asserts kept as defense-in-depth. Provable-red: dropping/renaming `success` or `timestamp`, or making `data.responsive` a non-bool, reds conforms_to; a bare 404/500 reds status_code + conforms_to.
